subject: 英語（中学）
question:
次の日記を読んで、問いに答えなさい。

Saturday, July 5

Today was a great day. In the morning, I went to the park with my dog, Max. We played for about an hour. Then I ate lunch with my family at home. In the afternoon, my friend Ken came to my house and we played video games together. It was really fun.

I had a math test today. I studied hard last night, but the test was very difficult. After school, I talked with my teacher, Ms. Ito. She said, "Don't worry. You can try again." I felt better after that.

When Keiko was twelve, she received a second-hand violin from her aunt. It was old, but it had a warm, deep sound. She practiced every day, even though it was hard at first. Three years later, she performed at the city music hall. Many people cried when they heard her play. Keiko thought, "Music can connect people's hearts."
---

question:
次の文章を読んで、問いに答えなさい。

Ryu grew up in a small fishing village. He watched his father go out to sea every morning. His father always said, "The sea gives us life, so we must take care of it." When Ryu was in high school, he saw a lot of plastic trash on the beach. He started a beach cleanup club at his school. Soon, thirty students joined. Ryu hoped that his small action would inspire others to protect the ocean.

Oscar stopped, helped the boy up, and finished the race last. His coach said, "That was the best thing I ever saw."

As she grew up, she learned that her grandfather had spent an entire week on that chair. He had painted a tiny bird on the back of it. Yoshiko kept the chair in her room. Every time she looked at it, she felt her grandfather's love.

One afternoon, a classmate named Lucas sat next to him and started drawing in a sketchbook. Shin loved drawing, too. Without words, they drew together every day. By spring, they were close friends. Shin learned that friendship doesn't always need words.

次の文章を読んで、問いに答えなさい。

Her neighbor, a ten-year-old girl named Hana, noticed and started watering the flowers every morning before school. Mrs. Tanaka watched from the window and smiled. When she recovered, she taught Hana how to grow roses.

subject: 英語（中学）

次の文章を読んで、問いに答えなさい。

Daichi wanted to win the school speech contest. He wrote his speech about his experience volunteering at an animal shelter. He practiced in front of the mirror every night. On the day of the contest, his heart was beating fast. He remembered why he wrote the speech—not to win, but to share something important. He spoke slowly and clearly. He did not win first place, but many students came to him afterward and said, "I want to volunteer too."

---
Kai was the best student in his class, and everyone expected him to win the science fair. He built a water filter using recycled materials. On the day of the fair, his filter worked, but another student's project—a seed-growing experiment—won first place. Kai was surprised. The judges said, "This project shows that small actions can help the environment in everyday life." Kai went home and thought about those words for a long time.
---

審査員が1位の作品を選んだ理由として最も適切なものはどれですか？

choices.label: A
choices.text: 日常生活の中で環境に役立つ小さな行動を示していたから。

choices.label: B
choices.text: リサイクル素材を使った技術力が評価されたから。

choices.label: C
choices.text: カイの水フィルターよりも見た目が美しかったから。

choices.label: D
choices.text: 科学的な実験方法が最も正確だったから。
